_deprecated: DEPRECATED: Narrative content (chars, laws, events, bundesrat) now served from API /api/content/game?locale=en. This file kept for UI strings (onboarding, setup, etc.) until full migration. See SMA-257.
_institutionConvention: Bundesrat → Bundesrat (Federal Council); Bundestag → Bundestag (Federal Parliament); Bundesland/Länder → Federal States

kanzler.name: Chancellor
kanzler.bio: Leads the coalition with a pragmatic course. Holds the threads together — but expects loyalty in return.

bonus.desc: Gives +5 parliament votes for all laws

fm.name: Robert Lehmann
fm.bio: Strict budget guardian of the CDU. Consistently opposes expensive reforms — but is usually right.

wm.name: Petra Maier
wm.bio: Pragmatic and solution-oriented. Important ally — maintains contacts with industry and state premiers.

im.name: Klaus Braun
im.bio: Conservative maverick. Fundamentally distrusts the coalition — actively seeks reasons to torpedo it.

bonus.desc: Stabilises Bundesrat (Federal Council) votes in 3 states

jm.name: Sara Kern
jm.bio: Lawyer with uncompromising principles. Blocks unconstitutional projects — but also protects the coalition from costly mistakes.

um.name: Jonas Wolf
um.bio: Drives climate policy forward, sometimes against the rest of the cabinet. Strong network among progressive voters.

am.name: Dr. Anna Schulze
am.role: Minister for Labour and Social Affairs
am.bio: Union-friendly and combative. Schulze knows the companies from the inside and consistently demands minimum wage and collective bargaining.

bonus.desc: GBD relationship regenerates passively when Mood ≥ 3

gm.name: Prof. Thomas Neumann
gm.bio: Clinical pragmatist. Neumann thinks in systems and seeks sustainable solutions for care and health insurance.

bonus.desc: Health field pressure rises more slowly when Mood ≥ 3

bm.name: Dr. Sabine Becker
bm.bio: Tireless reformer with federalism frustration. Becker wants to bring federal and state levels together — often in vain.

bonus.desc: Bundesrat blockade for education laws -15% when Mood ≥ 3

haushalt.title: Billion-euro hole in federal budget
haushalt.quote: "Unexpected tax shortfalls tear a hole of 14 billion euros."
haushalt.context: The Finance Ministry has revised estimates downward. Finance Minister Lehmann demands immediate countermeasures.
haushalt.ticker: Budget hole: Government under pressure — Lehmann demands austerity package

0.label: Push through austerity package
0.log: Austerity package passed. Budget stabilised, popularity drops.

1.label: Take on new debt
1.log: Debt brake stretched. Constitutional challenge threatens.

2.label: Announce tax increase
2.log: Tax increase announced. Business association protests.

skandal.title: Media reports on ministry waste
skandal.quote: "Der Spiegel reports on questionable consultancy contracts in the federal ministry."
skandal.context: The opposition demands resignation. Braun uses the opportunity to destabilise the coalition.
skandal.ticker: Spiegel report shakes coalition — opposition smells blood

0.label: Full transparency
0.log: Full disclosure. Short-term damage, long-term trust.

1.label: Distraction offensive
1.log: Distraction manoeuvre launched. Media sceptical.

2.label: Dismiss minister
2.log: Minister dismissed. Coalition partners angered.

euklage.title: Brussels initiates proceedings against Germany
euklage.quote: "The European Commission criticises the sluggish implementation of EU directives."
euklage.context: Three directives have not yet been transposed into national law. A fine of up to 500 million euros looms.
euklage.ticker: EU infringement proceedings against Germany opened

0.label: Rapid implementation
0.log: EU directives quickly implemented. Constitutional concerns remain.

1.label: Open EU negotiations
1.log: Negotiations with Brussels opened. Outcome open.

2.label: Include Bundesrat (Federal Council)
2.log: Regular procedure chosen. Fine risk remains.

konjunktur.title: ECB rate hike hits German economy
konjunktur.quote: "Investment collapses, short-time work rises — recession risk grows."
konjunktur.context: Maier warns against hasty spending cuts. Lehmann insists on fiscal discipline.
konjunktur.ticker: Economic downturn: Recession risk rises to 35%

0.label: Launch stimulus package
0.log: Stimulus package passed. Budget burdened, economy stabilised.

1.label: Wait and see
1.log: Wait-and-see stance. Economy worsens further.

2.label: Announce structural reforms
2.log: Structural reforms announced. Markets react positively.

koalition_krise.title: Coalition partner threatens to leave
koalition_krise.quote: "We can no longer support this policy", warns the coalition partner.
koalition_krise.context: Dispute over energy law escalates. The junior partner demands renegotiation of the coalition agreement.
koalition_krise.ticker: COALITION CRISIS: Junior partner issues ultimatum to Chancellery

0.label: Renegotiate
0.log: Coalition agreement renegotiated. Reform watered down.

1.label: Risk confrontation
1.log: Coalition crisis escalates. Early elections discussed.

2.label: Offer trade-off
2.log: Trade-off agreed. Coalition temporarily saved.

demo.title: Major demonstration shakes Berlin
demo.quote: "500,000 people on the streets — the largest demo in years."
demo.context: The protest targets social inequality. Progressives are mobilised but also dissatisfied with the government.
demo.ticker: Berlin: Historic demonstration for social justice

0.label: Offer dialogue
0.log: Dialogue with demonstrators offered. Progressive milieu calmed.

1.label: Reject demands
1.log: Demands rejected. Progressives disappointed.

2.label: Announce emergency programme
2.log: Emergency programme announced. Broad approval.

eufoerder.title: EU releases billion-euro structural funds
eufoerder.quote: "Brussels provides 4.8 billion euros for German climate investments."
eufoerder.context: A rare piece of good news: EU funds arrive earlier than expected. How will they be used?
eufoerder.ticker: EU releases 4.8 bn euros for Germany — government decides use

0.label: Climate & infrastructure
0.log: EU funds used for climate investments.

1.label: Prioritise housing
1.log: EU funds strengthen housing offensive.

2.label: Debt repayment
2.log: Debt repaid. Budget improved, room for reforms.

verfassungsgericht.title: Federal Constitutional Court strikes down key regulation
verfassungsgericht.quote: "The Federal Constitutional Court declares a core government law unconstitutional."
verfassungsgericht.context: The judges in Karlsruhe see fundamental rights violated. The opposition triumphs, the coalition must respond.
verfassungsgericht.ticker: Karlsruhe strikes down government law — coalition under pressure

0.label: Respect ruling
0.log: Ruling accepted. Trust in rule of law strengthened.

1.label: Introduce amendment
1.log: Legislative amendment in progress. Constitutionally corrected.

2.label: Consider constitutional change
2.log: Constitutional amendment announced. Jurists and opposition alarmed.

naturkatastrophe.title: Century flood devastates southern Germany
naturkatastrophe.quote: "The worst flood catastrophe in decades claims dozens of lives and causes billions in damage."
naturkatastrophe.context: Entire towns are underwater. Emergency services are at their limit. The federal government must act fast.
naturkatastrophe.ticker: Flood catastrophe: Federal government activates crisis team

0.label: Emergency aid package
0.log: Emergency aid package passed. Fast help for those affected.

1.label: Coordinate army deployment
1.log: Army in relief deployment. Population grateful.

2.label: State matter — wait
2.log: Federation points to state responsibility. Massive criticism follows.

cyberangriff.title: Massive cyber attack paralyses federal ministries
cyberangriff.quote: "A coordinated hacker attack has compromised central IT systems of the federal administration."
cyberangriff.context: Intelligence agencies suspect a state actor. Sensitive data may have been leaked.
cyberangriff.ticker: Cyber attack on federal government — IT systems paralysed

0.label: Crisis team & IT offensive
0.log: IT crisis team established. Cybersecurity becomes top priority.

1.label: Let intelligence investigate
1.log: Investigation initiated. Public learns little.

2.label: Diplomatic escalation
2.log: Diplomatic confrontation. International tensions rise.

g7_krise.title: G7 summit overshadowed by global tensions
g7_krise.quote: "International conflicts overshadow the G7 summit under German presidency."
g7_krise.context: The world is watching Berlin. A summit failure would be a diplomatic disaster.
g7_krise.ticker: G7 summit in Berlin: Chancellor under international pressure

0.label: Take mediator role
0.log: Germany as mediator. International reputation rises.

1.label: Demonstrate alliance loyalty
1.log: Alliance loyalty demonstrated. Partner state satisfied.

2.label: Prioritise German interests
2.log: National interests emphasised. International criticism grows.

whistleblower.title: Official reveals systematic surveillance
whistleblower.quote: "A senior official publishes explosive documents about illegal surveillance programmes."
whistleblower.context: The revelations hit the domestic intelligence service hard. Civil rights advocates demand consequences.
whistleblower.ticker: Surveillance scandal: Civil rights vs. security

0.label: Investigation committee
0.log: Investigation committee established. Inquiry begins.

1.label: Protect whistleblower
1.log: Whistleblower placed under protection. Intelligence service outraged.

2.label: Emphasise security interests
2.log: Security over transparency. Civil rights advocates protest.

streikwelle.title: Unions call for general strike
streikwelle.quote: "Public transport, daycare, hospitals — a strike wave paralyses public life."
streikwelle.context: Wage negotiations have failed. Unions demand double-digit pay rises.
streikwelle.ticker: General strike paralyses Germany — millions affected

0.label: Offer mediation
0.log: Mediation proceedings initiated. Social partners at the table.

1.label: Support employer side
1.log: Employer position strengthened. Unions threaten escalation.

2.label: Strengthen legal framework
2.log: Labour law reform announced. Long-term solution in sight.

wohnungsnot.title: Tens of thousands protest against soaring rents
wohnungsnot.quote: "In 50 cities, people take to the streets — affordable housing is the central demand."
wohnungsnot.context: Rents in major cities have doubled in 10 years. Families are being pushed to the outskirts.
wohnungsnot.ticker: Housing protests in 50 cities — government under pressure

0.label: Tighten rent cap
0.log: Rent cap tightened. Tenant associations satisfied, investors angered.

1.label: Promote social housing
1.log: Social housing programme launched. Effects visible in years.

2.label: Emphasise market solutions
2.log: Market should fix it. Protests intensify.

rechtsextremismus.title: Right-wing extremist attack shakes the nation
rechtsextremismus.quote: "A right-wing motivated attack claims victims and triggers a wave of solidarity and fear."
rechtsextremismus.context: The attack is part of a series of right-wing violence. The question of security agencies' failure looms.
rechtsextremismus.ticker: Right-wing extremist attack: Germany mourns and debates

0.label: Action plan against the far right
0.log: Action plan against right-wing extremism passed.

1.label: Strengthen security agencies
1.log: Intelligence and police forces expanded.

2.label: Differentiated analysis
2.log: Wait-and-see stance. Opposition accuses of inaction.

fachkraeftemangel.title: Skills shortage threatens economic competitiveness
fachkraeftemangel.quote: "800,000 vacancies cannot be filled — companies warn of relocation."
fachkraeftemangel.context: Trades, IT, care — workers are lacking everywhere. Business associations demand an offensive.
fachkraeftemangel.ticker: Skills shortage at record high — business sounds alarm

0.label: Immigration offensive
0.log: Skilled immigration facilitated. Business relieved.

1.label: Training initiative
1.log: Training offensive launched. Effects visible in years.

2.label: Let the market sort it out
2.log: Wait-and-see stance. Skills shortage worsens.

energiekrise.title: Energy price shock hits industry and consumers
energiekrise.quote: "Gas prices triple overnight — industry scales back production."
energiekrise.context: International supply bottlenecks drive prices. Consumers protest, companies threaten relocation.
energiekrise.ticker: Energy prices explode — Economy Minister warns of deindustrialisation

0.label: Introduce price brake
0.log: Energy price brake takes effect. Budget heavily burdened.

1.label: Industry subsidies
1.log: Industry subsidies passed. Jobs secured.

2.label: Accept market prices
2.log: Market prices enforced. Social hardship follows.

rentendebatte.title: Pension insurance reports billion-euro deficit
rentendebatte.quote: "The baby boomers are retiring — the system faces collapse."
rentendebatte.context: The pension report shows a growing deficit. Younger generations fear for their retirement.
rentendebatte.ticker: Pension insurance: Deficit grows — reform debate ignites

0.label: Raise retirement age
0.log: Retirement age raised to 68. Massive union protests.

1.label: Raise contributions
1.log: Pension contributions rise. Net wages drop slightly.

2.label: Introduce capital funding
2.log: Stock-based pension introduced. Conservatives sceptical.

pandemie_vorbereitung.title: WHO warns of new virus with pandemic potential
pandemie_vorbereitung.quote: "A new virus variant is spreading rapidly in Asia — the WHO calls for preparation."
pandemie_vorbereitung.context: Germany has learned from the last pandemic — or has it? Hospitals report bottlenecks.
pandemie_vorbereitung.ticker: WHO warning: New pandemic possible — Germany prepares

0.label: Prepare prevention package
0.log: Pandemic prevention package passed. Population feels protected.

1.label: Strengthen WHO cooperation
1.log: International cooperation expanded. Wait-and-see in Germany.

2.label: Wait and observe
2.log: Wait-and-see stance. Critics warn of negligence.

infrastruktur_kollaps.title: Bridge closures paralyse traffic
infrastruktur_kollaps.quote: "Dozens of dilapidated motorway bridges must be closed — traffic collapses."
infrastruktur_kollaps.context: Infrastructure has been neglected for decades. Truck detours cost the economy billions.
infrastruktur_kollaps.ticker: Infrastructure crisis: Crumbling bridges paralyse traffic

0.label: Emergency infrastructure programme
0.log: Infrastructure emergency programme launched. Billions for bridges and roads.

1.label: Support municipalities
1.log: Municipal infrastructure aid passed.

2.label: Prioritise and plan
2.log: Master plan presented. Critics demand immediate action.

migration_krise.title: Refugee numbers surge dramatically
migration_krise.quote: "Municipalities report overload — reception capacities are exhausted."
migration_krise.context: New conflicts drive hundreds of thousands to Europe. Local politicians demand federal help.
migration_krise.ticker: Refugee crisis: Municipalities at limit — federal government under pressure

0.label: Humanitarian reception
0.log: Humanitarian reception organised. Society divided.

1.label: Demand EU distribution
1.log: EU distribution mechanism demanded. Brussels reacts slowly.

2.label: Strengthen border controls
2.log: Border controls tightened. EU Commission examines proceedings.

verfassungsklage_schulden.title: Opposition sues over debt policy
verfassungsklage_schulden.quote: "The opposition has filed a complaint with the Federal Constitutional Court — violation of the debt brake."
verfassungsklage_schulden.context: The complaint has prospects of success. A ruling could overturn the budget.
verfassungsklage_schulden.ticker: Constitutional complaint against debt policy — government in trouble

0.label: Present supplementary budget
0.log: Supplementary budget presented. Debt brake complied with.

1.label: Declare emergency
1.log: Emergency declared. Jurists doubt justification.

2.label: Sit out complaint
2.log: Complaint ignored. Constitutional Court angered.

wiederaufbau_debatte.title: Dispute over reconstruction costs after flood
wiederaufbau_debatte.quote: "Who pays the 30 billion euros for reconstruction? Federation and states argue."
wiederaufbau_debatte.context: Flood victims wait for help while Berlin and state capitals debate responsibilities.
wiederaufbau_debatte.ticker: Reconstruction dispute: Federation vs. states — flood victims wait

0.label: Levy solidarity surcharge
0.log: Solidarity surcharge for reconstruction. Taxpayers protest.

1.label: Establish special fund
1.log: Special fund for reconstruction passed.

2.label: Introduce insurance mandate
2.log: Mandatory insurance against natural disasters introduced.

diplomatische_krise.title: Accused state retaliates with countermeasures
diplomatische_krise.quote: "In response to public accusations, the accused state imposes economic sanctions."
diplomatische_krise.context: Trade relations are at risk. Business urges de-escalation.
diplomatische_krise.ticker: Diplomatic crisis escalates — economic sanctions threaten

0.label: De-escalation
0.log: Diplomatic channels reopened. Crisis defused.

1.label: Counter-sanctions
1.log: Counter-sanctions imposed. Economy suffers.

2.label: Demand EU solidarity
2.log: EU partners asked for solidarity. Mixed reactions.

opposition_vorwurf.title: Opposition accuses government of inaction
opposition_vorwurf.quote: "After the right-wing extremist attack, the opposition accuses the government of negligent inaction."
opposition_vorwurf.context: A vote of no confidence is in the air. Media report intensively on security agency failures.
opposition_vorwurf.ticker: Opposition attacks government — no-confidence vote discussed

0.label: Announce immediate measures
0.log: Immediate measures against right-wing extremism announced.

1.label: Call vote of confidence
1.log: Vote of confidence called. High-risk manoeuvre.

2.label: Lead parliamentary debate
2.log: Parliamentary debate on internal security. Measured tone.

gewerkschaft_eskalation.title: Unions extend strike indefinitely
gewerkschaft_eskalation.quote: "After mediation fails, unions call for indefinite strikes."
gewerkschaft_eskalation.context: Airports, ports and hospitals are affected. Economic damage runs into billions.

0.label: Force wage agreement
0.log: Compulsory arbitration ordered. Unions outraged, strike ended.

1.label: Offer compromise
1.log: Compromise found. Wages rise, budget burdened.

2.label: Hold firm
2.log: Government holds course. Economic damage grows.

fm_ultimatum.title: Lehmann issues budget ultimatum
fm_ultimatum.quote: "Not a single law without full funding proof — or I resign."
fm_ultimatum.context: Finance Minister Lehmann is at the end of his patience. Too many expensive projects without funding.
fm_ultimatum.ticker: CABINET CRISIS: Finance Minister Lehmann threatens resignation

0.label: Promise funding proof
0.log: Funding pledge made. Lehmann appeased, reforms restricted.

1.label: Publicly contradict
1.log: Public dispute with Lehmann. Coalition destabilised.

2.label: Call special meeting
2.log: Coalition summit at Chancellery. Compromise found.

braun_ultimatum.title: Braun sabotages Bundesrat (Federal Council) vote
braun_ultimatum.quote: "I have advised the state interior ministers to vote against this law."
braun_ultimatum.context: Braun has acted behind the coalition's back and influenced three state interior ministers.
braun_ultimatum.ticker: Interior Minister Braun sabotages coalition partner in Bundesrat (Federal Council)

0.label: Publicly reprimand Braun
0.log: Braun officially reprimanded. Internal tension at maximum.

1.label: Include Braun
1.desc: Gives him influence, costs reform substance
1.log: Braun receives concessions. Coalition stabilised, reform watered down.

2.label: Dismiss Braun
2.log: Braun dismissed. Cabinet reshuffled.

wolf_ultimatum.title: Wolf threatens resignation over climate policy
wolf_ultimatum.quote: "If the energy law is watered down further, I can no longer represent this cabinet."
wolf_ultimatum.context: Wolf is frustrated with sluggish climate policy.
wolf_ultimatum.ticker: Environment Minister Wolf issues climate ultimatum to Chancellery

0.label: Prioritise energy law
0.log: Energy law put on priority list. Wolf stays.

1.label: Persuade Wolf to stay
1.log: Personal talk with Wolf. Provisional agreement.

2.label: Accept resignation
2.log: Wolf resigns. Progressive voters feel betrayed.

kern_ultimatum.title: Kern blocks law over constitutional concerns
kern_ultimatum.quote: "I will not sign any law that cannot withstand the Federal Constitutional Court."
kern_ultimatum.context: Kern has stopped the emergency law on the EU directive. She is substantively right — but time is running out.
kern_ultimatum.ticker: Justice Minister stops emergency law — constitutional concerns in cabinet

0.label: Adapt law to constitution
0.log: Law revised. Constitutionally sound but delayed.

1.label: Appoint expert commission
1.log: Constitutional commission appointed. Kern cooperates.

2.label: Apply political pressure
2.log: Kern feels overruled. Constitutional challenge threatens.

kanzler_ultimatum.title: Chancellor considers vote of confidence
kanzler_ultimatum.quote: "If the coalition cannot find a common line, I will call a vote of confidence."
kanzler_ultimatum.context: Internal divisions have become public. The Chancellery wants a clear commitment.
kanzler_ultimatum.ticker: Chancellor considers vote of confidence — coalition under pressure

0.label: Call coalition summit
0.log: Coalition summit creates new unity.

1.label: Risk vote of confidence
1.log: Vote of confidence called. Coalition holds narrowly.

kohl_bundesrat_sabotage.title: Kohl requests mediation committee
kohl_bundesrat_sabotage.quote: "Without concessions to the East I will request every mediation committee."
kohl_bundesrat_sabotage.context: Matthias Kohl (Ostblock) has independently filed a mediation committee request. The law will be delayed by at least 2 months.
kohl_bundesrat_sabotage.ticker: Kohl requests mediation committee — law delayed

0.label: Accept delay
0.log: Mediation committee procedure running. Vote delayed.

1.label: Address Kohl directly
1.log: Talk with Kohl. Relationship improved, delay remains.

2.label: Publicly criticise
2.log: Kohl publicly criticised. Ostblock hardens position.

wm_ultimatum.title: Maier threatens resignation over business location policy
wm_ultimatum.quote: "The combination of tax increase and regulation drives companies abroad."
wm_ultimatum.context: Maier sees economic policy on the wrong track.
wm_ultimatum.ticker: Economics Minister Maier issues business location ultimatum

0.label: Accelerate tax reform
0.log: Tax reform brought forward. Economy stabilised, budget burdened.

1.label: Roll back regulation
1.desc: Popular with industry, unpopular with progressives
1.log: Regulation pause decided. Industry relieved.

2.label: Outvote Maier
2.log: Coalition discipline enforced. Maier complies.

am_ultimatum.title: Schulze demands minimum wage emergency programme
am_ultimatum.quote: "Without an emergency programme for the minimum wage I can no longer represent this cabinet."
am_ultimatum.context: Schulze is at the end of her patience. The unions expect action.
am_ultimatum.ticker: CABINET CRISIS: Labour Minister Schulze threatens resignation

0.label: Approve emergency programme
0.log: Minimum wage emergency programme approved. Schulze appeased.

1.label: Offer negotiation
1.log: Coalition summit on social policy. Compromise found.

2.label: Reject
2.log: Demand rejected. Schulze threatens resignation.

gm_ultimatum.title: Neumann demands recognition of care emergency
gm_ultimatum.quote: "If we do not officially recognise the care emergency, I lose all credibility."
gm_ultimatum.context: Neumann sees health policy at a crossroads.
gm_ultimatum.ticker: CABINET CRISIS: Health Minister Neumann threatens resignation

0.label: Recognise care emergency
0.log: Care emergency officially recognised. Neumann stays.

1.label: Appoint expert commission
1.log: Commission appointed. Neumann provisionally satisfied.

2.label: Ignore
2.log: Demand ignored. Neumann threatens resignation.

koalitionsbruch.title: Coalition partner threatens to leave
koalitionsbruch.quote: "Without tangible concessions we cannot continue this coalition."
koalitionsbruch.context: The relationship with the coalition partner is at a critical low. You have 3 months to respond.
koalitionsbruch.ticker: Coalition crisis: Partner threatens to leave

0.label: Call coalition summit
0.log: Coalition summit called. Relationship improved.

1.label: Buy time
1.log: Negotiations continue.

koalitionskrise_ultimatum.title: Partner demands adherence to coalition agreement
koalitionskrise_ultimatum.quote: "We signed an agreement. Current policy deviates too much."
koalitionskrise_ultimatum.context: The coalition agreement score is critical. The partner demands more congruence with the agreed course.

0.label: Adjust course
0.log: Course adjusted. Partner appeased.

1.label: Stand firm
1.log: No concessions. Tension remains.

bm_ultimatum.title: Becker demands education emergency
bm_ultimatum.quote: "Federalism blocks every reform. Without recognition of the emergency I am out."
bm_ultimatum.context: Becker is frustrated by the lack of progress in education policy.
bm_ultimatum.ticker: CABINET CRISIS: Education Minister Becker threatens resignation

0.label: Recognise education emergency
0.log: Education emergency recognised. Becker stays.

1.label: Announce federalism reform
1.log: Federalism reform announced. Becker provisionally satisfied.

2.label: Reject
2.log: Demand rejected. Becker threatens resignation.

ee.desc: Doubling of expansion targets, simplified permitting.

wb.desc: 400,000 new apartments p.a., building law reform.

bp.desc: Federal school funding via cooperation article.

sprecher.name: Petra Schulz
sprecher.bio: Pragmatic and cooperative. Reliable base but sensitive when federal policy overrides state competencies.
sprecher.quote: The North needs reliable partners in Berlin — we are ready.

koal_hafen.label: Port infrastructure in stimulus package
koal_hafen.desc: Northern German ports receive additional funds — burdens budget.

koal_bildung_veto.label: Education package with state veto rights
koal_bildung_veto.desc: Federal States receive extended veto rights on education decisions.

koal_strukturfonds.label: Increase structural fund allocations
koal_strukturfonds.desc: More EU structural funds for northern German regions.

sprecher.name: Hans Brenner
sprecher.bio: The decisive swing voter. Open to deals but always negotiates. Unpredictable without quid pro quo.
sprecher.quote: Nothing goes without something in return — you know that.

mitte_digitalsteuer.label: No digital tax on SMEs
mitte_digitalsteuer.desc: Exempt medium-sized companies from digital tax.

mitte_weinbau.label: Wine-growing exception in energy law
mitte_weinbau.desc: Wine-growing receives special rules on energy requirements.

mitte_kommune.label: Double municipal infrastructure funds
mitte_kommune.desc: Doubling of federal funds for municipal infrastructure.

sprecher.name: Edmund Huber
sprecher.bio: Ideological opponent of the coalition. Fundamentally votes against Red-Green — exception: when a law strengthens state rights.
sprecher.quote: Federalism first — everything else is negotiable.

kons_foederal.label: Constitutional federalism guarantee
kons_foederal.desc: Anchor federalism more strongly in constitution.

kons_auto.label: Automotive industry exception in EE law

sprecher.name: Matthias Kohl
sprecher.bio: The unpredictable one. Structural change trauma shapes all decisions. Eastern investments open him — ignore him and he becomes an active saboteur.
sprecher.quote: The East was forgotten too often — that changes now.

ost_invest.label: Eastern Germany investment programme (€3 bn)
ost_invest.desc: Special programme for eastern German structural support.

ost_kohle.label: Delay coal phase-out by 3 years
ost_kohle.desc: Longer transition periods for eastern German coal regions.

ost_abschreibung.label: Special depreciation for eastern German companies
ost_abschreibung.desc: Tax incentives for investments in the East.

koalitionstreue.name: Stefan Möller
koalitionstreue.bio: Pragmatic port politician. Focus on infrastructure and trade.
koalitionstreue.quote: The North needs investments — we negotiate.

pragmatische_mitte.name: Claudia Vogt
pragmatische_mitte.quote: Nothing works without us — everyone knows that.

konservativer_block.name: Franz Bauer
konservativer_block.bio: Federalism purist. State rights above all.
konservativer_block.quote: The federation should mind its own business.

ostblock.name: Ingrid Weber
ostblock.bio: Structural change expert. Demands equivalent living conditions.

laenderfinanzausgleich.title: State financial equalisation dispute escalates
laenderfinanzausgleich.quote: "Pragmatic Centre and Ostblock demand redistribution of financial flows — or blockade."
laenderfinanzausgleich.context: Factions Brenner and Kohl have allied. They demand a revision of state financial equalisation. Without concessions, blockade threatens in all consent-required laws.
laenderfinanzausgleich.ticker: State financial equalisation: Brenner and Kohl demand redistribution

0.label: Make concessions
0.log: Concessions on state financial equalisation. Brenner satisfied, budget burdened.

1.label: Reject
1.log: Demands rejected. Brenner and Kohl angered.

2.label: Commission expert report
2.log: Expert report commissioned. Topic postponed for now.

landtagswahl.title: State election topples faction
landtagswahl.quote: "Surprising change of government — the political map shifts."
landtagswahl.context: A state election has changed the governing party in a federal state. The faction composition in the Bundesrat (Federal Council) changes. The affected faction must renegotiate.
landtagswahl.ticker: State election: Change of government alters Bundesrat (Federal Council) majorities

0.label: Accept new majorities
0.log: Change of government accepted. Faction composition updated.

kohl_eskaliert.title: Kohl escalates — special session convened
kohl_eskaliert.quote: "Without concessions to the East I will request every mediation committee."
kohl_eskaliert.context: Matthias Kohl has filed a mediation committee request for an ongoing law. The law will be delayed by 2 months. How do you respond?
kohl_eskaliert.ticker: Kohl requests mediation committee — law delayed

0.label: Cooperate
0.log: Cooperation with Kohl. Relationship improved, delay remains.

1.label: Block legally
1.log: Legal review initiated. Delay remains, Kohl angered.

2.label: Publicly criticise
2.log: Kohl publicly criticised. Ostblock hardens position.

sprecher_wechsel.title: New faction speaker in Bundesrat (Federal Council)
sprecher_wechsel.quote: "The faction has elected a new speaker — with different priorities."
sprecher_wechsel.context: A faction speaker has been replaced. The new character has different interests and a new trade-off pool. The relationship must be rebuilt.
sprecher_wechsel.ticker: Speaker change: Faction with new face

0.label: Include new speaker
0.log: New speaker acknowledged. Relationship building required.

bundesrat_initiative.title: Federal States bring own law
bundesrat_initiative.quote: "The Federal States want not just to react — they want to shape."
bundesrat_initiative.context: A faction has introduced its own law. You must decide: approval strengthens the relationship, blockade costs sympathy and popularity.
bundesrat_initiative.ticker: Bundesrat (Federal Council) initiative: Federal States demand say

0.label: Approve
0.log: Bundesrat (Federal Council) initiative approved. Relationship to faction improved.

1.label: Block
1.log: Bundesrat (Federal Council) initiative blocked. Faction angered, popularity drops.

foederalismusgipfel.title: Federalism summit — all speakers at one table
foederalismusgipfel.quote: "Historic opportunity: All four faction speakers received simultaneously."
foederalismusgipfel.context: At the federalism summit you can cultivate all four factions simultaneously — at reduced cost of 8 PK per faction instead of 15.
foederalismusgipfel.ticker: Federalism summit: All faction speakers in Berlin

0.label: Cultivate all four factions
0.desc: 32 PK (8 per faction), relationship +3 for all
0.log: Federalism summit: Relationship to all factions improved.

1.label: Invite only Centre and Ostblock
1.desc: 16 PK, relationship +3 for Brenner and Kohl
1.log: Federalism summit with Brenner and Kohl. Relationship improved.

2.label: Cancel summit
2.log: Federalism summit cancelled. Chance missed.

haushaltsdebatte.prioritaetenDesc: Choose 2 policy areas for additional spending (+10 bn.)
haushaltsdebatte.partnerKeineForderung: No specific demand from coalition partner.
haushaltsdebatte.forderungErfuelllt: The demand was met by your priority choice.
haushaltsdebatte.lehmannUeberschuss: The budget is solid. We have room for investments.
haushaltsdebatte.lehmannAusgeglichen: Balanced budget — but no room for experiments.
haushaltsdebatte.lehmannDefizit: The deficit is concerning. Every expenditure must be justified.

haushalt.title: Budget
haushalt.desc: Revenue, mandatory spending and law costs. Balance affects economic situation.
haushalt.steuerquoteDesc: 1× per year, 15 PK. Increase: BdI -8, GBD +5. Decrease: BdI +8, GBD -8.

gesetz.blockiertVerfassungsgericht: Blocked by ongoing constitutional court proceedings
gesetz.ausgeschlossen: Not possible: You have already passed {{gesetz}}

gesetzAgenda.title: Law Agenda
gesetzAgenda.keineVorbereitung: Choose a law to start the legislature ({{count}} laws)
gesetzAgenda.kongruenzTooltip: How well this law matches your political orientation (0–100 scale). Higher means closer alignment with your government line. This is not the direct Bundestag vote probability.
gesetzAgenda.scoreTip: Recommendation score: {{score}}/100 — how advisable it is to introduce this law now (KPI need, Bundestag outlook, fit with your alignment, efficiency). Scale 0–100.
gesetzAgenda.filterAlleTooltip: No status filter: all laws visible at your complexity level.
gesetzAgenda.filterEntwurfTooltip: Only laws in draft (not yet introduced).
gesetzAgenda.filterEingebrachtTooltip: Only laws already in the parliamentary process.
gesetzAgenda.filterAktivTooltip: Only laws with an ongoing process (e.g. committee, vote).
gesetzAgenda.filterBlockiertTooltip: Only laws that are currently blocked.
gesetzAgenda.filterBeschlossenTooltip: Only laws that have already been passed.
gesetzAgenda.sortStandardTooltip: Sort policy fields and laws by congruence with your party alignment (best match first). “All” is the status filter; “Standard” controls ordering within the agenda.
gesetzAgenda.sortEmpfohlenTooltip: Sort laws within each policy field by recommendation score (KPI need, success outlook, congruence, efficiency).

kabinett.title: Cabinet
kabinett.desc: Your cabinet and coalition partner. Mood and loyalty affect government work.

bundestag.title: Bundestag
bundestag.desc: Seat distribution and ongoing votes in parliament.
bundestag.nfDisclaimer: The NF stands outside democratic cooperation. No coalition, no talks, no lobbying.
bundestag.ersterTabHinweis: The Bundestag reflects political reality. The National Front (NF) is present in parliament but outside any democratic cooperation.
bundestag.logNfMedienkritik: Criticism: law aligns with NF positions — media climate strained.

media.title: Media
media.desc: Media climate, public opinion and voter milieus. Media climate affects how laws are perceived.

arbeit.name: Working-class milieu
arbeit.desc: Union-aligned voters, focus on social justice.

media.akteureSub: Sentiment and reach of key formats — the media index is derived from these.

aktionen.title: Targeted media work
aktionen.sub: Each action can be used once every 3 months (separate cooldown per action).

social.title: Social media campaign
social.risiko: 15% backlash risk: −20 sentiment if it backfires

qualitaet.title: Quality press briefing
qualitaet.bedingung: Only with a stable budget (balance > −25 bn €)

kommunal_klima_initiative.title: Hamburg and Munich launch independent climate programme
kommunal_klima_initiative.quote: “We won't wait any longer for the federal government.”
kommunal_klima_initiative.context: Major cities have independently adopted climate protection measures. Pressure from civil society and post-materialist milieus is high.
kommunal_klima_initiative.ticker: Hamburg and Munich: Own climate programme — federal government under pressure

0.label: Praise as role model
0.desc: +2% parliament votes for matching laws, no PK cost.
0.log: Municipal initiative praised as role model. Positive signal effect.

1.label: Coordinate and launch pilot
1.desc: 8 PK — full municipal pilot with the matching law.
1.log: Federal government coordinates municipal initiative. Pilot project launched.

2.label: Ignore
2.log: Municipal initiative ignored. Discontent in the cities.

kommunal_sozial_initiative.title: Ruhr area cities demand minimum wage increase — now
kommunal_sozial_initiative.context: Industrial cities in the Ruhr area have launched municipal minimum wage initiatives. The social centre feels abandoned by federal politics.
kommunal_sozial_initiative.ticker: Ruhr area: Cities demand minimum wage increase — government under observation

0.label: Praise as role model
0.desc: +2% parliament votes for matching laws, no PK cost.
0.log: Municipal initiative praised as role model.

1.label: Coordinate and launch pilot
1.log: Federal government coordinates. Pilot project launched.

2.label: Ignore
2.log: Municipal initiative ignored.

kommunal_sicherheit_initiative.title: Cities demand more police presence — independent measures
kommunal_sicherheit_initiative.quote: “The federal government won't act — we must.”
kommunal_sicherheit_initiative.context: Municipalities have independently announced security measures. Traditional voters demand more capability from the federal government.
kommunal_sicherheit_initiative.ticker: Municipalities launch own security initiatives — federal government responds

0.label: Praise as role model
0.desc: +2% parliament votes for matching laws, no PK cost.
0.log: Municipal initiative praised as role model.

1.label: Coordinate and launch pilot
1.log: Federal government coordinates. Pilot project launched.

2.label: Ignore
2.log: Municipal initiative ignored.

vorstufe_kommunal_erfolg.title: Pilot project [city name]: Success confirmed
vorstufe_kommunal_erfolg.quote: “The model works — time for the federal level.”
vorstufe_kommunal_erfolg.context: The municipal pilot project was successful. The experience confirms effectiveness — time for nationwide implementation.
vorstufe_kommunal_erfolg.ticker: Municipal pilot successful — signal for the federal government

0.label: Acknowledge
0.desc: Success confirmed. Bonus will be applied.
0.log: Pilot project success acknowledged.

vorstufe_laender_erfolg.title: [Federal State] passes [law] — signal for federal level
vorstufe_laender_erfolg.context: A federal state has successfully implemented the law as a pilot. The signal effect for other states and the federal level is significant.
vorstufe_laender_erfolg.ticker: State pilot successful — other states are watching

0.label: Invite more states
0.desc: 12 PK — starts second state pilot with +10% success chance.
0.log: More states invited to pilot.

1.label: Acknowledge
1.log: State pilot success acknowledged.

ebene.eu: Advance laws via EU level. Longer duration but bypasses Bundesrat (Federal Council).
ebene.land: Launch state pilot projects. Medium duration and costs.
ebene.kommune: Use municipal alliances. Fastest alternative with lower costs.
ebene.potentialHint: Select a law to start a pilot project.
ebene.staedtebuendnisDesc: 10 PK — Increases chance for municipal initiatives.
ebene.kommunalKonferenzDesc: 8 PK, 1×/year — Reduces pressure in 2 policy fields.

onboarding.willkommen: Welcome to Politikpraxis. You take over the Chancellery — 48 months until the next election.
onboarding.parteiTitle: Which party do you lead into government?
onboarding.partei_sdp: You lead the Social Democratic Party into government. Labour, social affairs and justice are at the core.
onboarding.partei_cdp: You lead the Christian Democratic Party into government. Economy, security and family are at the core.
onboarding.partei_ldp: You lead the Liberal Democratic Party into government. Freedom, digitalisation and personal responsibility are at the core.
onboarding.partei_lp: You lead the Left Party into government. Redistribution, peace and basic income are at the core.
onboarding.partei_gp: You lead the Green Party into government. Climate, environment and sustainability are at the core.
onboarding.partnerHint: With your political orientation, {{partei}} is your most likely coalition partner.
onboarding.headlineMeta:
40.2% · SPD/Greens coalition likely
Turnout: 76.3% · Historic night in Berlin
onboarding.kabinettText: Your cabinet. Six personalities, six portfolios.
onboarding.memo:
TO: Federal Chancellery
FROM: Coalition working group
RE: Coalition agreement — open points

The draft coalition agreement is ready.
{{lawCount}} draft laws are prioritised.
Political capital: {{pk}} units available.{{brLine}}

The work begins now.
onboarding.memoBrLine: First Bundesrat (Federal Council) session: in 3 months.
onboarding.erster_monat: First month in office. The coalition is sworn in, ministries are staffed. Now the work begins.
onboarding.erstes_gesetz: Your first law. Political capital (PK) is your central resource — for introducing, lobbying and votes.
onboarding.bundesrat_intro: The Bundesrat (Federal Council) is active. 16 states, 4 factions. Lobbying affects willingness to vote.
onboarding.skandal_intro: A political scandal has reached the government. How do you respond?
onboarding.wahlkampf_beginn: The campaign begins. 6 months until the election — time for speeches, coalition actions and media offensives.

gameSetup.title: Game Start
gameSetup.subtitle: Configure your candidacy
gameSetup.complexity4Bullet2: EU events, follow-up events, media agenda

credits.title: Credits
credits.disclaimer: Politikpraxis is a fictional politics simulation game for educational purposes. All depicted persons, parties and institutions are fictitious. Politikpraxis does not load any external resources; all fonts and map data are served locally.

gegenfinanzierung.title: Counter-financing required
gegenfinanzierung.desc: The law "{{gesetz}}" causes significant budget strain. How should it be financed?
gegenfinanzierung.kostenHinweis: This law costs {{kosten}}/year. How should it be financed?
gegenfinanzierung.ressortDesc: Cuts in one ministry cover the costs.
gegenfinanzierung.steuergesetzDesc: The law only takes effect once the tax law is passed.

agenda.title: Legislative agenda
agenda.brVoteIn: Bundesrat (Federal Council) vote in {{months}} months — lobbying active

partnerWiderstand.messageHinweis: {{partner}} has concerns. If you introduce it anyway, coalition relationship drops by {{malus}}.
partnerWiderstand.messageWiderstand: {{partner}} warns: introducing it would strain the coalition strongly (−{{malus}} relationship).
partnerWiderstand.messageVeto: {{partner}} blocks this bill until there is agreement. A coalition round (15 PC) can lift the veto.
partnerWiderstand.pkZuWenig: Not enough political capital (15 for coalition round).
partnerWiderstand.vetoFreigabe: Coalition round held — you can now introduce the bill.

bundesrat.title: Bundesrat (Federal Council)
bundesrat.subtitle: 4 factions represent 16 Federal States. Lobbying influences voting readiness.
bundesrat.aggregiertUnterstuetzen: {{prozent}}% of federal states support this law.
bundesrat.bilateraleHint: Maintain ties with individual states (10 political capital, +10 relationship).
bundesrat.keineAbstimmung: No law in Bundesrat (Federal Council) procedure yet. Once a law passes the Bundestag, the Bundesrat will vote.
bundesrat.keineAbstimmungLobbyHint: No law in Bundesrat procedure yet
bundesrat.lobbyingGesperrt: Lobbying blocked: Relationship under 20. Repair required.
bundesrat.bilateraleDesc: Strengthen the relationship with individual federal states (10 PK, +10 relation).

endScreen.wonSubtitle: You won the election with {{percent}}% approval.
endScreen.lostSubtitle: The legislative period ends with {{percent}}% approval — below the required majority.
endScreen.lostMisstrauen: Vote of no confidence! Six months below 20% approval lead to the fall of the government.
endScreen.lostKoalitionsbruch: Coalition collapse! The coalition has broken apart.

auswertung.noteHint: Overall grade from five dimensions (democracy, economy, society, communication, efficiency).
auswertung.communityHint: Aggregated opt-in data only — no personal data.
auswertung.optIn: I want to contribute to anonymous community statistics. No personal data is transmitted.

medienklima.label: Media climate
medienklima.tooltipHint: Affects election chances and communication actions
medienklima.caption: The media climate affects your election chances and the impact of communication actions. Values ≥ 60 boost your forecast, values < 40 endanger it.

verbaende.title: Associations
verbaende.subtitle: Business associations and interest groups. Conversations and trade-offs affect relationships and policy field pressure.

framing.title: How do you communicate this law?

pressemitteilung.title: Press release
pressemitteilung.desc: Choose a topic for your press release (5 PK).

monatszusammenfassung.title: Month {{monat}} completed
monatszusammenfassung.subtitle: At a glance — what happened?
monatszusammenfassung.settingAutoShow: Show monthly summary after each month
monatszusammenfassung.medienTooltipStimmungGut: From the government’s perspective: helpful — sentiment at this outlet moved in your favour.
monatszusammenfassung.medienTooltipStimmungSchlecht: From the government’s perspective: harmful — sentiment at this outlet moved against you.
monatszusammenfassung.medienTooltipReichweiteGut: From the government’s perspective: helpful — this channel’s reach shrinks (less anti-establishment influence).
monatszusammenfassung.medienTooltipReichweiteSchlecht: From the government’s perspective: harmful — reach grows; anti-establishment media gain influence.

legislaturBilanz.beat3Desc: Choose 2 core themes for your campaign communication. Green = credible, Yellow = risky.

wahlkampf.title: Campaign
wahlkampf.tvDuellBanner: TV debate this or next month!

pk.explanation: Your action resource. Every action costs PK. Regenerates monthly based on your approval.

zustimmung.explanation: How popular your government is. At the end of the legislative period (Month 48) you must exceed the election threshold to win.

kongruenz.explanation: How well a law matches your ideological orientation (0–100%). High congruence = less PK cost, low congruence = more effort and resistance.

arbeitslosigkeit.explanation: KPI: Unemployment rate in %. Low = good. Influenced by economic drift and laws.

haushaltssaldo.explanation: KPI: Revenue minus expenditure in %. Positive = surplus. Negative = deficit. Influenced by laws and economic conditions.

gini.explanation: KPI: Measure of inequality (0–100). Low = less inequality. Influenced by social policy.

zufriedenheit.explanation: KPI: General public satisfaction (0–100%). High = good. Influenced by all KPIs and laws.

koalition.explanation: How stable your governing coalition is (0–100%). Calculated from cabinet mood and partner loyalty. Below 15% → Coalition collapse = game over!

spielraum.explanation: Budget funds available for new laws. Derived from revenue minus mandatory spending minus ongoing costs.

schuldenbremse.explanation: From level 3: Constitutional limit on new debt. Violations risk austerity measures and loss of confidence.

medienklima.explanation: How positive media coverage is (0–100). Influences PK costs for laws and campaign success.

milieus.explanation: Voter groups with different ideological profiles. Their approval determines your election forecast.

kvScore.explanation: How many points of the coalition agreement are fulfilled. High score = more stable coalition, low score = partner becomes dissatisfied.

vorstufen.explanation: Municipal or state pilots before introducing a law. Grant bonuses on parliament votes, PK costs and Bundesrat approval.

bundesrat.explanation: 4 factions represent 16 federal states. Laws with a state tag require Bundesrat majority. Relationship building and lobbying influence the vote.

erstesGesetz.title: Your first law
erstesGesetz.text: Select a law from the Agenda and click “Introduce” to bring it to parliament. Each introduction costs Political Capital (PK).

pkKnapp.title: PK running low
pkKnapp.text: Your Political Capital regenerates monthly based on your approval. Higher approval = more PK per month. Set priorities!

koalitionsSpannungen.title: Coalition tensions
koalitionsSpannungen.text: Your coalition stability is dropping. Watch your cabinet members’ mood and the relationship with the coalition partner. Concessions and conversations help.

haushaltsDefizit.title: Budget deficit
haushaltsDefizit.text: Your budget is in the red. Expensive laws strain your fiscal headroom. Watch the cost traffic light on laws: Green = manageable, Yellow = noticeable, Red = risky.

bundesratInfo.title: Bundesrat vote
bundesratInfo.text: A law has passed parliament and goes to the Bundesrat. Use lobbying with the factions to secure the necessary votes.

wahlkampfStart.title: Campaign begins!
wahlkampfStart.text: The last 6 months of the legislative period! Use speeches, coalition actions and the media offensive to improve your election forecast. The TV debate is coming soon too.

geschwindigkeit.title: Game speed
geschwindigkeit.text: Press Space to pause/resume. Use keys 1 and 3 to control speed. The game pauses automatically for important events.

kabinettIntro.title: Your cabinet
kabinettIntro.text: Your ministers have their own mood and loyalty. Happy ministers give bonuses — unhappy ones issue ultimatums. Click a minister for details.

gesetzWirkung.title: Laws take time
gesetzWirkung.text: Passed laws don't take effect immediately — effects kick in after a few months. Plan ahead and introduce laws early!

haushaltErklaerung.title: The budget
haushaltErklaerung.text: Every law has running costs. Watch the budget balance — excessive deficit triggers the debt brake and limits your room to act.

medienklimaIntro.title: Media climate
medienklimaIntro.text: The media climate affects public perception. Scandals lower it, good news raises it. From level 3 you can use press releases.

euRouteInfo.title: Alternative routes
euRouteInfo.text: A law was blocked? You can push it through via the EU, state pilots, or municipalities on an alternative path. Each route has its own pros and cons.

verbaendeIntro.title: Associations & interest groups
verbaendeIntro.text: Associations like the BDI, trade unions, or environmental groups have their own interests. Good relations can help with votes — but concessions come at a cost.

ministerAgenda.title: Minister initiatives
ministerAgenda.text: Your ministers pursue their own agendas and make demands. Fulfil them for better mood — or refuse at the risk of an ultimatum.

halbzeit.title: Halfway through
halbzeit.text: Half your term is over. Check your approval ratings and the budget. 24 months to the election — now is the best time for major reforms.

koalitionPanel.beziehungKritischHint: Relationship critical! Coalition meeting or concession urgently recommended.
koalitionPanel.koalitionsbruchDroht: Coalition break imminent! Act now — coalition round or concession!
koalitionPanel.beziehungAngespannt: Relationship strained — regular coalition rounds stabilise the partnership.

vorbereitungModal.laenderPilotTooltip: State pilot: pilot project with one Bundesrat faction. Costs {{cost}} political capital, runs {{duration}} months. On success +10% yes votes in the Bundestag; you pick the faction in the dialog.

wirtschaft.sektorKrise: Sector in crisis — associations are mobilising.

tooltip.bip: Annual economic growth. Positive means expansion, negative means recession.
tooltip.inflation: Annual price increase. The target is around 2%. High inflation erodes purchasing power.
tooltip.arbeitslosigkeit: Share of people out of work. Below about 5% is considered low.
tooltip.investitionsklima: How attractive Germany is for investment. Affects long-term growth.

medienAkteure.warnVolatil: Very volatile — sentiment can shift quickly

agenda.oeffentlich: Balanced and independent. Responds to substance, not PR.
agenda.boulevard: Outrage sells. Scandals are amplified strongly.
agenda.qualitaet: Fact-based. Responds positively to sound fiscal policy.
agenda.social: Algorithm-driven. Very volatile — trends shift quickly.
agenda.konservativ: National-conservative agenda. Barely influenced by PR.
agenda.alternativ: Distrusts everything established. Grows when media climate is poor.

kpiVerlauf.noDataGlobal: KPI trends are shown from the second month.
kpiVerlauf.title: KPI trends
kpiVerlauf.subtitle: Last {{count}} months

al.label: Unemployment
al.desc: Share of unemployed in the labour force.
al.impact: High unemployment lowers satisfaction and endangers re-election.

hh.label: Budget balance
hh.desc: Difference between government revenue and spending.
hh.impact: A persistent deficit triggers the debt brake and reduces future room to act.

gi.label: Gini coefficient
gi.desc: Measure of economic inequality (0 = equal, 100 = maximum inequality).
gi.impact: High inequality worsens the society dimension and weakens progressive milieus.

zf.label: Satisfaction
zf.desc: General population satisfaction with the government.
zf.impact: Low satisfaction lowers the election forecast directly and reduces PK regeneration.

pendingEffekte.title: Planned effects — overview
pendingEffekte.nextHint: Next effect in {{offset}}. Hover for details.

hh.label: Budget
hh.desc: Budget balance in bn € — higher is better

bewertungRadar.hint: Hover for details on each dimension

demokratie.label: Democracy
demokratie.desc: Coalition stability and fulfilment of the coalition agreement

wirtschaft.label: Economy
wirtschaft.desc: Budget balance, unemployment and economic index

effizienz.label: Efficiency
effizienz.desc: Laws passed relative to political capital spent
